title: Walking into the room wearing the thing you bought yourself
subtitle: A visualisation for practising visible wealth in a safe imagined space.
excerpt: A short visualisation practice for Day 18 of the MONEY program. Walk through the experience of entering a room visibly, comfortably, while wearing or carrying something you bought for yourself without apology.
sourceNotes: Original to homemade.education as a companion visualisation for Day 18 of MONEY: A 12-Week Tapping Program (Rebecca J Page, 2025). The visualisation format draws on standard mental rehearsal practice used in performance psychology.

content.text: A five-minute visualisation for working with money visibility, in a safe imagined space. The image is simple. You walk into a room. You wear or carry something you bought without apology. You feel easy about it.
Sit comfortably. Close your eyes or soften your gaze. Take three slow breaths.

Choose a specific thing you bought for yourself, or something you'd buy if money weren't a worry. A coat, a bag, a piece of jewellery, a pair of shoes. Something you'd wear on your body rather than hide away.

Picture yourself wearing it, or carrying it. Now picture yourself walking through a door into a room, a room with people in it. People who might notice. Picture yourself walking in, and your body staying easy. Not apologising with your posture. Not covering up the thing or making it smaller. Just walking in.
Sit with that picture for a minute. If discomfort rises, notice where in your body it sits. Let it be there without changing it.
Now run the picture again: you walk in, easy. The room continues. Nothing bad happens. You take up your normal amount of space.
Open your eyes when ready. If a line came up that felt true or false, write it down.

Original to homemade.education as a companion piece for Day 18 of MONEY: A 12-Week Tapping Program (Rebecca J Page, 2025). Mental rehearsal like this is the same kind of work used in sports and stage practice.
